question.text: An investor wants to exit Sovereign Gold Bonds before maturity. After which year can the investor exercise the early redemption option, and when?

answer.text: After the 3rd year, on any business day
After the 5th year, on interest payment dates
After the 5th year, on any business day with 30 days' notice
After the 4th year, on the anniversary of the issue date only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SGBs have a tenure of 8 years, but early redemption is permitted after the 5th year from the date of issue. This early exit can only be exercised on interest payment dates (i.e., semi-annual dates). The investor cannot exit on any arbitrary date - it must coincide with the coupon payment schedule. **The Trap:** "After the 5th year, on any business day" sounds almost correct but misses the critical restriction that exit is only on interest payment dates. The 3rd year and 4th year options are factually incorrect. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A tests lock-in periods and exit mechanisms for various investment products. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients needing liquidity before 5 years can sell SGBs on the stock exchange (if listed), but this forfeits the maturity capital gains exemption. Always map client liquidity needs before recommending SGBs.
